preamble: The shift from the lateral approximant [l] to the back/central unrounded vowel [ʌ] (the 'strut' or 'caret' vowel) is a common form of L-vocalization in Southeast England. It is particularly prominent in syllabic contexts where the [l] functions as the nucleus of the syllable.

examples.note: The final syllabic lateral is replaced by a vowel approaching the STRUT space.
In rapid speech, the dark L can vocalize into a central-back unrounded quality.
